title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
What is Insurance? Types, Benefits & Why It Matters
Considering the uncertainties of life, ensuring financial protection is important to avoid unnecessary hassle during tough times. With insurance, you can shield yourself, your loved ones, and your possessions from unforeseen dangers or losses. Insurance provides financial support when you need it most, whether it be in the event of a medical emergency, an accident, damage to the house, or the untimely death in the family.
To maximize the benefits, it is essential to understand what insurance is, how it functions, and its relevance. Alongside, we will also discuss different types of insurance serving different objectives.
What Is Insurance?Insurance is an agreement between you and an insurance company in which you pay a small fixed amount regularly, called a premium. In return, the company promises to give you the agreed sum insured or the sum assured in the case of the respective covered risks. The risks can be any unfortunate incident which affects your life, like an illness, an accident, damage to your car or house, or even the death of the life assured. Insurance supports financially, acting as a financial backup. There are multiple insurance plans designed for your different needs, like life insurance, health insurance, and car insurance.
How Does Insurance Work?In the simplest terms, an insurance plan transfers the financial risk of unexpected losses from an individual or entity to an insurance company. First, you choose and purchase a plan as per your requirements. In this regard, you need to pay premiums, monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, annually or in one go (lump sum amount). In return, the insurance company agrees to pay the sum assured or sum insured against the covered risks, like hospital costs, accidental losses, or even the death of the life insured.
What Are the Types of Insurance Available in India?
Life Insurance
General Insurance
Life Insurance
Life insurance is a contract between you and an insurance company where the insurer agrees to pay a nominated beneficiary a sum of money upon the death of the life insured, in exchange for premium payments. It is an insurance product that provides a safety net for your loved ones, ensuring they are protected from financial difficulties in the event of your absence. Let us understand this in detail with various types available.
Term Insurance
Term insurance is the simplest form of life insurance. It covers the life insured for a fixed tenure. If the life assured dies during that time, the nominee gets the sum assured.
Whole Life Insurance
Whole life insurance gives coverage to the life assured for the entire life, not just for a predetermined period of time. When the life insured dies, the insurance company gives the sum assured to the nominee. The policyholder pays premiums regularly, but the good part is that this plan lasts a lifetime.
Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP)
ULIP is a unique product that combines life insurance with investment. Part of your premium goes towards life cover, and the other part is invested in market-linked funds like equity or debt. This plan offers security to your family, along with growing your funds over time.
Term Insurance with Return of Premium (TROP)A term insurance plan gives the nominee the sum assured if the life insured dies during the policy term. However, if the life assured survives the term, there are no payments made from the insurers end. This is where a TROP turns out to be useful. It returns all the premiums paid (excluding taxes), even if the life insured survives the tenure.
Endowment PlanAn endowment plan offers both life insurance and savings. The policyholder pays a premium for a set time. If the life insured dies during the policy term, the nominee gets the full sum assured. If the life assured survives the term, the nominee gets a lump sum of money at maturity.
Child PlanA child insurance plan helps you save for your child’s future, such as their college education or marriage expenses. The parent is the life insured, and the child is the beneficiary/nominee. In case of your death, the plan continues, and the nominee/beneficiary (here, your child) receives the sum assured.
General InsuranceGeneral insurance protects things other than life, like your health, home, car, or travel. It is also known as non-life insurance. It is a broad category of insurance that provides financial protection against various risks and losses not related to life. They include accidents, theft, hospital stays, or natural disasters. This type of insurance has further different types serving different objectives.
Health InsuranceHealth insurance helps cover the expenses related to a medical condition of the life insured, which is included in the policy coverage. It helps to cover costs like hospital and medical bills. If the life insured falls sick, needs surgery, or faces an emergency, this plan covers the cost of treatment.
Home Insurance
Home insurance offers coverage against damages to your house and its contents against risks like fire, flood, theft, earthquake, and so on. If something unfortunate happens to your dwelling, the insurer would provide payment to repair the damage to your home or to rebuild your home as covered under the policy.
Travel InsuranceTravel insurance helps protect you during trips, whether in India or abroad. If you face problems like flight delay, lost check-in baggage, passport loss, or a medical emergency while travelling, this plan covers the cost. Some plans also give support for hospitalization during the trip. It is useful for family vacations, business travel, or when you study abroad.
Pet InsurancePet insurance covers veterinary and other costs if your dog, cat, or any other pet falls ill or gets injured. It can also cover vet visits, vaccinations, and surgeries. Some plans pay if your pet is lost or stolen. You pay a small premium regularly for the coverage. If your pet needs treatment, the insurance helps with bills. Just like we protect our family, this plan protects our furry friends.
Electronic Equipment InsuranceThis insurance protects your mobile phone or laptop from accidental damage, theft and certain electrical and mechanical damages. If your device gets damaged or lost, the plan covers repair or replacement costs. You pay a small premium to keep the device covered.
Car InsuranceIn India, it is also the law that every vehicle must have at least third-party car insurance. Car insurance is a plan that protects you and your vehicle from risks like an accident, theft, or losses to third parties due to your car. If your car gets damaged or you hurt someone else in an accident, car insurance helps pay for the repair costs or medical bills. This type of insurance pays the other person if their car is hit by you. It covers injuries to the vehicle as well as the person.
Features of Insurance
Insurance comes with many features that help protect your money and give you peace of mind.
One main feature is the premium—this is the amount you pay regularly to keep the plan running. It can be paid monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or lump sum.
Another feature is the sum assured (life insurance) or sum insured (general insurance)—this is the amount the insurance company agrees to pay when you raise a valid claim.
Every insurance policy also has a term, which is the time the plan stays active. Then, there is a waiting period, especially in health insurance, where you must wait before making some claims.
Some insurance plans also have a deductible, which is the amount you pay from your side before the company pays the rest. The policy document is an important paper that lists what is covered and what is not.
Lastly, some plans also offer tax benefits under Section 80C under the old tax regime.  Additionally, the claim amount is tax-free as per Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, subject to certain to subject and conditions..
Understanding these features can help you choose the right policy that fits your life and needs.
What are the Benefits of Insurance?
Insurance helps protect you and your family from sudden money troubles. One big benefit is financial safety. If you face a health problem, accident, or other big loss, insurance can help pay the bills. Another benefit is peace of mind. When you know that you and your loved ones are covered, you feel less worried. Insurance also helps in future planning. For example, life insurance helps your family by paying the sum assured to your nominee in the event of your ( life assured) untimely demise during the term. Health insurance pays for costly hospital bills. Some insurance plans also give tax benefits, which means you save money when filing your income tax. A few types of plans, like ULIPs or endowment policies, also help in growing your savings. It supports you financially and helps you live a more secure life.
Conclusion
Insurance is a tool that protects your money and secures your future. Whether it is health, life, or car-related problems, insurance helps reduce the burden. Knowing what insurance is  essential for everyone. With many plans available in India, you can choose one that fits your needs. Always read the terms and check what is covered and what is not.
FAQs
What is insurance in simple words?Insurance is like a safety net. You pay a small amount called a premium to an insurance company every month or year. In return, the company promises to pay for the loss or damage covered by the policy.
What are the factors that affect life insurance premiums?Life insurance premiums are the payments you make to keep your contract in force. Depending on many factors, premiums are priced differently. Generally, younger, healthier non-smokers will pay less in premiums than older individuals, those with more health concerns or smokers. In a nutshell, the factors are your age, gender, health, lifestyle, occupation, sum assured, maturity amount, payouts, and the length of the insurance term you want.
What is the waiting period under insurance policies?A waiting period means the time you are required to wait until you can use your insurance policy after purchasing it. For example, if you purchase a health insurance policy today, you may not be able to make a claim until the waiting period is over.
How many claims can I file under my insurance policy?You can file more than one claim. There is usually no strict limit, but each claim must match the rules of the policy. For example, if you have health insurance and fall sick twice in a year, you can claim both times if it is covered. But there is a total amount limit, called the sum insured. Once that amount is used up, you cannot claim more for that year.
What are the 7 fundamentals of insurance?These are 7 basic ideas that insurance is built on:
Insurable Interest – You must care about the person or thing you insure.
Utmost Good Faith – Be honest when buying insurance.
Indemnity – Insurance pays for your loss, not extra.
Contribution – If you have more than one policy, all share the payout.
Subrogation – The company can recover money from the person who caused the loss.
Proximate Cause – The main reason for the loss must be covered.
Loss Minimisation – You must try to reduce damage when a loss happens.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
What is Insurance? Types, Benefits & Why It Matters
Considering the uncertainties of life, ensuring financial protection is important to avoid unnecessary hassle during tough times. With insurance, you can shield yourself, your loved ones, and your possessions from unforeseen dangers or losses. Insurance provides financial support when you need it most, whether it be in the event of a medical emergency, an accident, damage to the house, or a untimely death in the family.
To maximize the benefits, it is essential to understand what insurance is, how it functions, and its relevance. Alongside, we will also discuss different types of insurance serving different objectives.
What Is Insurance? Insurance is an agreement between you and an insurance company in which you pay a fixed amount regularly, called a premium. In return, the Insurer pays the agreed sum insured or the sum assured when a covered risk occurs, as specified in the policy. . The risks can be any unfortunate incident which affects your life, like an illness, an accident, damage to your car or house, or even the death of the life assured. Insurance provides financial support, acting as a safety net during unforeseen events. There are multiple insurance plans designed for your different needs, like life insurance, health insurance, car insurance etc.
How Does Insurance Work?
In the simplest terms, an insurance plan transfers the financial risk of unexpected losses from an individual or entity to an insurance company. First, you choose and purchase a plan as per your requirements. In this regard, you need to pay premiums, monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, annually or in one go (lump sum amount). In return, the insurance company agrees to pay the sum assured or sum insured against the covered risks, like hospital costs, accidental losses, or even the death of the life assured .
What Are the Types of Insurance Available in India?
● Life Insurance ● General Insurance
1. Life Insurance
Life insurance is a contract between the policyholder and an insurance company where the insurer agrees to pay the nominee a sum of money upon the death of the life assured , in exchange for premium payments. Life insurance provides a financial safety net for your loved ones, ensuring they are protected from financial difficulties in the event of your absence. Let us understand this in detail with various types available.
Term Insurance
Term insurance is the simplest form of life insurance. It covers the life assured for a fixed tenure. If the life assured dies during that time, the nominee gets the sum assured.
Term Insurance with Return of Premium (TROP) A term insurance plan gives the nominee the sum assured if the life assured dies during the policy term. However, if the life assured survives the term, there is no maturity benefit in a term plan . This is where a TROP turns out to be useful. It returns all the premiums paid (excluding taxes), even if the life insured survives the tenure.
Whole Life Insurance
Whole life insurance gives coverage to the life assured for the entire life, not just for a predetermined period of time. When the life assured dies, the insurer pays the sum assured to the nominee.
Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP)
ULIP is a life insurance plan that combines life insurance with market linked investment. Part of your premium goes towards life cover, and the other part is invested in market-linked funds like equity funds , debt funds, balanced funds. This plan offers financial security to your family, and gives you the opportunity to grow funds over time .
Endowment Plan An endowment plan offers both life insurance and savings. The policyholder pays a premium for a set time. If the life assured dies during the policy term, the nominee gets the full sum assured. If the life assured survives the term, they get a lump sum of money at maturity.
Child Insurance Plan A child insurance plan helps you save for your child’s future, such as their college education or marriage expenses. The parent is the life assured , and the child is the nominee. In case of your demise, the plan continues, (if wavier of premium option is opted ) and the nominee (here, your child) receives the policy benefits .
2. General Insurance
General insurance protects things other than your life, like your health, home, car, or travel. It is also known as non-life insurance. It is a broad category of insurance that provides financial protection against various risks and losses not related to life. They include accidents, theft, hospital stays, or natural disasters. This type of insurance has different types serving different objectives.
Health Insurance Health insurance provides financial protection against medical expenses incurred due to illnesses or
health conditions covered under the policy. It helps pay for costs such as hospitalization, treatments, surgeries, and emergency care etc.
Home Insurance
Home insurance provides financial protection against damage to your house and its belongings caused by events such as fire, floods, theft, earthquakes, and other covered risks. In the event of an unfortunate incident, the insurer compensates for repairs or reconstruction of your home, as outlined in the policy terms.
Travel Insurance Travel insurance helps protect you during trips, whether in India or abroad. If you face problems like flight delay, lost check-in baggage, passport loss, or a medical emergency while travelling, this plan covers the cost. Some plans also give support for hospitalization during the trip. It is useful for family vacations, business travel, when you study abroad etc.
Pet Insurance Pet insurance typically covers veterinary and related expenses if your pet ( dog ,cat etc )falls ill or gets injured. While some policies may extend coverage to other pets like rabbits, guinea pigs, or exotic birds, such coverage is not always included and varies by insurer. It can also cover vet visits, vaccinations, and surgeries. Some plans pay if your pet is lost or stolen. You pay a small premium regularly for the coverage. If your pet needs treatment, the insurance helps with bills. Just like we protect our family, this plan protects our furry friends.
Electronic Equipment Insurance This insurance protects your mobile phone or laptop or any electronic gadget from accidental damage, theft and certain electrical and mechanical damages. If your device gets damaged or lost, the plan covers repair or replacement costs. You pay a regular premium to keep the device covered.
Motor Insurance In India, The Motor Vehicles Act of 1988 statesthat every vehicle must have third-party car insurance. Car insurance is a financial protection plan that covers you and your vehicle against risks such as accidents, theft, and damage to third parties. If your car is damaged or you cause injury or damage to another person or their property in an accident, the insurance helps cover repair costs, medical expenses etc. It typically includes coverage for both vehicle damage and bodily injury, depending on the type of policy you choose.
Features of Insurance
Insurance comes with many features designed to safeguard your finances and give you peace of mind during uncertain times.
 One main feature is the premium—this is the amount you pay regularly to keep the plan running. It can be paid monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or lump sum.  Another feature is the sum assured (life insurance) or sum insured (general insurance)—this is the amount the insurance company agrees to pay when you raise a valid claim.  Every insurance policy also has a term, which is the duration during which the plan stays active. Then, there is a waiting period, especially in health insurance, where you must wait before making some claims.  Some insurance plans such as health insurance also have a deductible, which is the portion of expenses you pay before the insurance company covers the remaining costs . The policy document is an important document that lists what is covered and what is not along with the terms of coverage.  Lastly, some plans also offer tax benefits under Section 80C under the old tax regime. Additionally, the claim amount is tax-free as per Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, subject to certain terms and conditions..
Understanding these features can help you choose the right policy that fits your life and needs.
What are the Benefits of Insurance?
Insurance provides financial security for you and your family by helping manage unexpected expenses during emergencies or unforeseen events.. One big benefit is financial safety. If you face a health problem, accident, loss of life or other covered substantial loss, the insurance payout can help pay the bills , etc and provide financial safety. Another benefit is peace of mind. When you know that you and your loved ones are covered, you feel less worried. Insurance also helps in future planning. For example, life insurance helps your family by paying the sum assured to your nominee in the event of your ( life assured) untimely demise during the term. Health insurance pays for hospital bills. Some insurance plans also give tax benefits, which means you save money when filing your income tax. It supports you financially and helps you live a more secure life.
Conclusion
Insurance acts as a financial safety net, helping protect your savings and secure your future. Whether it's health issues, life uncertainties, or car-related mishaps, insurance eases the financial burden during challenging times.. Knowing what insurance is is essential for everyone. With many plans available in India, you can choose one that fits your needs. Always read the terms and check what is covered and what is not.
FAQs
What is insurance in simple words? Insurance is like a safety net. You pay the amount called a premium to an insurance company at selected durations such as every month , year etc. In return, the Insurer promises to pay for the loss or damage covered by the policy.
What are the factors that affect life insurance premiums? Life insurance premiums are the payments you make to keep your contract in force. Depending on many factors, premiums are priced differently. Generally, younger, healthier non-smokers will pay less premiums than older individuals, those with more health concerns or smokers. A few of the factors that affect premiums include are your age, gender, health, lifestyle, occupation, sum assured, and the length of the insurance term you want.
What is the waiting period under insurance policies? A waiting period in insurance refers to the time span after purchasing the policy during which the coverage for certain conditions or events is not active. ..
How many claims can I file under my general insurance policy? You can file more than one claim in general insurance policy. There is usually no strict limit, but each claim must match the rules of the policy. For example, if you have health insurance and fall sick twice in a year, you can claim both times if it is covered. But there is a limit, called the sum insured. Once that amount is used up, you cannot claim more for that year.
What are the 6 fundamentals of insurance? These are 7 basic ideas that insurance is built on:
1. Insurable Interest – The policyholder must have a potential financial loss in the event of a
covered incident (like death or damage) to the insured item or individual. 2. Utmost Good Faith – It mandates that both the insurer and the insured must be completely
honest and disclose all material facts relevant to the policy . 3. Indemnity – Insurer agrees to compensate the insured for their loss.
4. Contribution – If you have multiple policies covering the same risk, all insurers share the claim
proportionally. 5. Subrogation – Under then fundamental of subrogation the insurance company can request
reimbursement from the at-fault party after it has paid a covered claim. 6. Proximate Cause – It is the primary or most dominant reason for a loss or damage that can be
directly linked to an insured event.

ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. changes.word_original: If the life insured dies during the policy term, the nominee gets the full sum assured.
changes.pdf_final: If the life assured dies during the policy term, the nominee gets the full sum assured.

changes.word_original: If the life assured survives the term, the nominee gets a lump sum of money at maturity.
changes.pdf_final: If the life assured survives the term, they get a lump sum of money at maturity.

changes.word_original: Child PlanA child insurance plan helps you save for your child’s future, such as their college education or marriage expenses.
changes.pdf_final: Child Insurance Plan A child insurance plan helps you save for your child’s future, such as their college education or marriage expenses.

changes.word_original: The parent is the life insured, and the child is the beneficiary/nominee.
changes.pdf_final: The parent is the life assured , and the child is the nominee.

changes.word_original: In case of your death, the plan continues, and the nominee/beneficiary (here, your child) receives the sum assured.
changes.pdf_final: In case of your demise, the plan continues, (if wavier of premium option is opted ) and the nominee (here, your child) receives the policy benefits .

changes.word_original: General InsuranceGeneral insurance protects things other than life, like your health, home, car, or travel.

changes.pdf_final: General Insurance
General insurance protects things other than your life, like your health, home, car, or travel.

changes.word_original: This type of insurance has further different types serving different objectives.
changes.pdf_final: This type of insurance has different types serving different objectives.

changes.word_original: Health InsuranceHealth insurance helps cover the expenses related to a medical condition of the life insured, which is included in the policy coverage.
changes.pdf_final: Health Insurance Health insurance provides financial protection against medical expenses incurred due to illnesses or

changes.word_original: It helps to cover costs like hospital and medical bills.
changes.pdf_final: health conditions covered under the policy.

changes.word_original: If the life insured falls sick, needs surgery, or faces an emergency, this plan covers the cost of treatment.
changes.pdf_final: It helps pay for costs such as hospitalization, treatments, surgeries, and emergency care etc.

changes.word_original: Home insurance offers coverage against damages to your house and its contents against risks like fire, flood, theft, earthquake, and so on.
changes.pdf_final: Home insurance provides financial protection against damage to your house and its belongings caused by events such as fire, floods, theft, earthquakes, and other covered risks.

changes.word_original: If something unfortunate happens to your dwelling, the insurer would provide payment to repair the damage to your home or to rebuild your home as covered under the policy.
changes.pdf_final: In the event of an unfortunate incident, the insurer compensates for repairs or reconstruction of your home, as outlined in the policy terms.

changes.word_original: Travel InsuranceTravel insurance helps protect you during trips, whether in India or abroad.
changes.pdf_final: Travel Insurance Travel insurance helps protect you during trips, whether in India or abroad.

changes.word_original: It is useful for family vacations, business travel, or when you study abroad.
changes.pdf_final: It is useful for family vacations, business travel, when you study abroad etc.

changes.word_original: Pet InsurancePet insurance covers veterinary and other costs if your dog, cat, or any other pet falls ill or gets injured.
changes.pdf_final: Pet Insurance Pet insurance typically covers veterinary and related expenses if your pet ( dog ,cat etc )falls ill or gets injured.
While some policies may extend coverage to other pets like rabbits, guinea pigs, or exotic birds, such coverage is not always included and varies by insurer.

changes.word_original: Electronic Equipment InsuranceThis insurance protects your mobile phone or laptop from accidental damage, theft and certain electrical and mechanical damages.
changes.pdf_final: Electronic Equipment Insurance This insurance protects your mobile phone or laptop or any electronic gadget from accidental damage, theft and certain electrical and mechanical damages.

changes.word_original: You pay a small premium to keep the device covered.
changes.pdf_final: You pay a regular premium to keep the device covered.

changes.word_original: Car InsuranceIn India, it is also the law that every vehicle must have at least third-party car insurance.
changes.pdf_final: Motor Insurance In India, The Motor Vehicles Act of 1988 statesthat every vehicle must have third-party car insurance.

changes.word_original: Car insurance is a plan that protects you and your vehicle from risks like an accident, theft, or losses to third parties due to your car.
changes.pdf_final: Car insurance is a financial protection plan that covers you and your vehicle against risks such as accidents, theft, and damage to third parties.

changes.word_original: If your car gets damaged or you hurt someone else in an accident, car insurance helps pay for the repair costs or medical bills.
changes.pdf_final: If your car is damaged or you cause injury or damage to another person or their property in an accident, the insurance helps cover repair costs, medical expenses etc.

changes.word_original: This type of insurance pays the other person if their car is hit by you.
changes.pdf_final: It typically includes coverage for both vehicle damage and bodily injury, depending on the type of policy you choose.

changes.word_original: It covers injuries to the vehicle as well as the person.

changes.word_original: Insurance comes with many features that help protect your money and give you peace of mind.
changes.pdf_final: Insurance comes with many features designed to safeguard your finances and give you peace of mind during uncertain times.

changes.word_original: One main feature is the premium—this is the amount you pay regularly to keep the plan running.
changes.pdf_final:  One main feature is the premium—this is the amount you pay regularly to keep the plan running.

changes.word_original: It can be paid monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or lump sum.
changes.pdf_final: It can be paid monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly, or lump sum.  Another feature is the sum assured (life insurance) or sum insured (general insurance)—this is the amount the insurance company agrees to pay when you raise a valid claim.  Every insurance policy also has a term, which is the duration during which the plan stays active.

changes.word_original: Another feature is the sum assured (life insurance) or sum insured (general insurance)—this is the amount the insurance company agrees to pay when you raise a valid claim.
changes.pdf_final: Then, there is a waiting period, especially in health insurance, where you must wait before making some claims.  Some insurance plans such as health insurance also have a deductible, which is the portion of expenses you pay before the insurance company covers the remaining costs .

changes.word_original: Every insurance policy also has a term, which is the time the plan stays active.
changes.pdf_final: The policy document is an important document that lists what is covered and what is not along with the terms of coverage.  Lastly, some plans also offer tax benefits under Section 80C under the old tax regime.

changes.word_original: Insurance helps protect you and your family from sudden money troubles.
changes.pdf_final: Insurance provides financial security for you and your family by helping manage unexpected expenses during emergencies or unforeseen events..

changes.word_original: If you face a health problem, accident, or other big loss, insurance can help pay the bills.
changes.pdf_final: If you face a health problem, accident, loss of life or other covered substantial loss, the insurance payout can help pay the bills , etc and provide financial safety.

changes.word_original: Health insurance pays for costly hospital bills.
changes.pdf_final: Health insurance pays for hospital bills.

changes.word_original: A few types of plans, like ULIPs or endowment policies, also help in growing your savings.

changes.word_original: Insurance is a tool that protects your money and secures your future.
changes.pdf_final: Insurance acts as a financial safety net, helping protect your savings and secure your future.

changes.word_original: Whether it is health, life, or car-related problems, insurance helps reduce the burden.
changes.pdf_final: Whether it's health issues, life uncertainties, or car-related mishaps, insurance eases the financial burden during challenging times..

changes.word_original: Knowing what insurance is  essential for everyone.
changes.pdf_final: Knowing what insurance is is essential for everyone.

changes.word_original: What is insurance in simple words?Insurance is like a safety net.
changes.pdf_final: What is insurance in simple words?

changes.word_original: You pay a small amount called a premium to an insurance company every month or year.
changes.pdf_final: Insurance is like a safety net.

changes.word_original: In return, the company promises to pay for the loss or damage covered by the policy.
changes.pdf_final: You pay the amount called a premium to an insurance company at selected durations such as every month , year etc.

changes.word_original: What are the factors that affect life insurance premiums?Life insurance premiums are the payments you make to keep your contract in force.
changes.pdf_final: In return, the Insurer promises to pay for the loss or damage covered by the policy.
What are the factors that affect life insurance premiums?
Life insurance premiums are the payments you make to keep your contract in force.

changes.word_original: Generally, younger, healthier non-smokers will pay less in premiums than older individuals, those with more health concerns or smokers.
changes.pdf_final: Generally, younger, healthier non-smokers will pay less premiums than older individuals, those with more health concerns or smokers.

changes.word_original: In a nutshell, the factors are your age, gender, health, lifestyle, occupation, sum assured, maturity amount, payouts, and the length of the insurance term you want.
changes.pdf_final: A few of the factors that affect premiums include are your age, gender, health, lifestyle, occupation, sum assured, and the length of the insurance term you want.

changes.word_original: What is the waiting period under insurance policies?A waiting period means the time you are required to wait until you can use your insurance policy after purchasing it.
changes.pdf_final: What is the waiting period under insurance policies?

changes.word_original: For example, if you purchase a health insurance policy today, you may not be able to make a claim until the waiting period is over.
changes.pdf_final: A waiting period in insurance refers to the time span after purchasing the policy during which the coverage for certain conditions or events is not active. ..

changes.word_original: How many claims can I file under my insurance policy?You can file more than one claim.
changes.pdf_final: How many claims can I file under my general insurance policy?
You can file more than one claim in general insurance policy.

changes.word_original: But there is a total amount limit, called the sum insured.
changes.pdf_final: But there is a limit, called the sum insured.

changes.word_original: What are the 7 fundamentals of insurance?These are 7 basic ideas that insurance is built on:
changes.pdf_final: What are the 6 fundamentals of insurance?

changes.word_original: Insurable Interest – You must care about the person or thing you insure.
changes.pdf_final: These are 7 basic ideas that insurance is built on:

changes.word_original: Utmost Good Faith – Be honest when buying insurance.
Indemnity – Insurance pays for your loss, not extra.
changes.pdf_final: Insurable Interest – The policyholder must have a potential financial loss in the event of a

changes.word_original: Contribution – If you have more than one policy, all share the payout.
changes.pdf_final: covered incident (like death or damage) to the insured item or individual. 2.

changes.word_original: Subrogation – The company can recover money from the person who caused the loss.
changes.pdf_final: Utmost Good Faith – It mandates that both the insurer and the insured must be completely

changes.word_original: Proximate Cause – The main reason for the loss must be covered.
changes.pdf_final: honest and disclose all material facts relevant to the policy . 3.

changes.word_original: Loss Minimisation – You must try to reduce damage when a loss happens.
changes.pdf_final: Indemnity – Insurer agrees to compensate the insured for their loss.
Contribution – If you have multiple policies covering the same risk, all insurers share the claim
proportionally. 5.
Subrogation – Under then fundamental of subrogation the insurance company can request
reimbursement from the at-fault party after it has paid a covered claim. 6.
Proximate Cause – It is the primary or most dominant reason for a loss or damage that can be
directly linked to an insured event.
